purpose: Selection-oriented rebuild of the PNG gallery manifest. Artifacts remain examples; capability records carry chart-selection semantics.

cohort_and_population.label: Cohort and population
cohort_and_population.description: Compare stable populations, new/lost cohorts, or retention/churn-style contribution.

lost_cohort_contribution.best_when: The reader needs to see contribution by lost or last-active cohort.
lost_cohort_contribution.avoid_when: Avoid for simple time trend or non-cohort composition.

since_cohort_contribution.best_when: The reader needs to see contribution by first-active cohort over periods.
since_cohort_contribution.avoid_when: Avoid for simple time trend or non-cohort composition.

same_population_total_change.best_when: The reader needs total change on a stable like-for-like population.
same_population_total_change.avoid_when: Avoid when population churn or mix composition is the main finding.

same_population_composition_change.best_when: The reader needs like-for-like total movement and composition together.
same_population_composition_change.avoid_when: Avoid when the population is not stable or exact detail is required.

composition_and_mix.label: Composition and mix
composition_and_mix.description: Show part-to-whole structure, nested categories, mix shift, or two-dimensional composition.

trend_with_cumulative_or_share_area.best_when: The reader needs a time-series area view for absolute contribution or share over periods.
trend_with_cumulative_or_share_area.avoid_when: Avoid when precise line trajectory or side-by-side period gaps are clearer.

width_metric_times_height_metric.best_when: The reader needs variable-width categories where width is an additive quantity, area is an additive value, and height is their derived ratio.
width_metric_times_height_metric.avoid_when: Avoid without a meaningful additive width denominator, when area is not additive, or when the derived ratio encoding would be hard to read.

same_population_composition_change.best_when: The reader needs like-for-like total movement and composition together.
same_population_composition_change.avoid_when: Avoid when the population is not stable or exact detail is required.

two_dimension_share_and_size.best_when: The reader needs to see composition across two categorical dimensions where both width and height carry meaning.
two_dimension_share_and_size.avoid_when: Avoid when categories are too many, exact comparison matters, or the question is a simple ranking.

composition_within_ranked_totals.best_when: The reader needs both total size and component composition across categories.
composition_within_ranked_totals.avoid_when: Avoid when exact component comparison or time trend is more important than composition.

composition_change_over_periods.best_when: The reader needs to see total and mix composition across periods or comparable scopes.
composition_change_over_periods.avoid_when: Avoid when exact values or simple trend shape matters more than composition.

concentration_with_component_breakdown.best_when: The reader needs Pareto concentration plus stacked composition by another dimension or class.
concentration_with_component_breakdown.avoid_when: Avoid when a plain Pareto or plain stacked bar would be easier to read.

distribution.label: Distribution
distribution.description: Show shape, spread, density, cumulative share, or individual observations for a metric.

spread_and_outliers_summary.best_when: The reader needs median, quartiles, spread, and outliers, especially across groups.
spread_and_outliers_summary.avoid_when: Avoid when distribution shape details or individual observations matter.

cumulative_distribution_and_percentiles.best_when: The reader needs percentile thresholds or cumulative share below/above values.
cumulative_distribution_and_percentiles.avoid_when: Avoid when frequency bins or individual observations are easier for the reader.

frequency_shape.best_when: The reader needs to see bins, skew, modal ranges, or rough frequency shape for one metric.
frequency_shape.avoid_when: Avoid when exact percentile comparison or individual points are needed.

smoothed_distribution_shape.best_when: The reader needs a smoothed view of distribution shape across one or more groups.
smoothed_distribution_shape.avoid_when: Avoid when sample size is small or exact bins/observations are important.

individual_observations.best_when: The reader needs to see individual observations, density, and outliers without summarizing them away.
individual_observations.avoid_when: Avoid with too many points or when an aggregate distribution shape is enough.

evidence_and_reporting_tables.label: Evidence and reporting tables
evidence_and_reporting_tables.description: Provide exact values, statement rows, funnel stages, attribute evidence, product support, or visibility detail.

current_vs_emerging_signal_alignment.best_when: The reader needs to compare current winners with emerging signals and see alignment or divergence.
current_vs_emerging_signal_alignment.avoid_when: Avoid when only one cohort layer exists.

bundle_share_and_index_evidence.best_when: The reader needs exact bundle evidence for current winners or emerging signals.
bundle_share_and_index_evidence.avoid_when: Avoid for product-level validation or shelf visibility decomposition.

product_level_grounding.best_when: The reader needs product-level support for selected bundles or standout examples.
product_level_grounding.avoid_when: Avoid for ranking category-wide signal prevalence.

rank_weighted_visibility.best_when: The reader needs visibility, incremental lane contribution, or alpha robustness evidence.
rank_weighted_visibility.avoid_when: Avoid as demand, sales, or causality evidence.

stage_counts_and_conversion.best_when: The reader needs exact stage counts, conversion rates, and drop-offs.
stage_counts_and_conversion.avoid_when: Avoid when a visual shape is more important than exact stage evidence.

summary_exact_values.best_when: The reader needs compact exact AC/PY totals and deltas by comparison row.
summary_exact_values.avoid_when: Avoid when the message depends on the period path or monthly sequencing.

structured_statement_values.best_when: The reader needs a P&L-style table with business rows and comparison columns.
structured_statement_values.avoid_when: Avoid for exploratory charting or relationship analysis.

metric_relationship.label: Metric relationship and secondary metrics
metric_relationship.description: Show relationships, outliers, or secondary metric markers across observations.

total_plus_related_marker.best_when: The reader needs total metric movement with a related marker shown in the same compact view.
total_plus_related_marker.avoid_when: Avoid when the relationship between metrics is the main analytical object.

primary_rank_plus_secondary_marker.best_when: The reader needs ranked contribution plus a related non-additive marker such as price, margin percentage, or growth.
primary_rank_plus_secondary_marker.avoid_when: Avoid when the secondary metric deserves a full relationship plot or when composition is the point.

two_metric_relationship_plus_size.best_when: The reader needs x/y relationship plus magnitude encoded by bubble size.
two_metric_relationship_plus_size.avoid_when: Avoid when bubble size would obscure the relationship or when period movement is the question.

relationship_between_two_metrics.best_when: The reader needs to see association, clusters, quadrants, or outliers between two metrics.
relationship_between_two_metrics.avoid_when: Avoid for time trends, one-dimensional ranking, or exact tables.

ranking_and_comparison.label: Ranking and comparison
ranking_and_comparison.description: Compare categories, entities, or periods by value, gap, rank, or compact summary.

ranked_single_metric_comparison.best_when: The reader needs a sorted comparison of one additive metric across items for one selected scope or period.
ranked_single_metric_comparison.avoid_when: Avoid when composition, hierarchy, time movement, or a second metric relationship is the point.

dimension_period_values_and_delta.best_when: The reader needs one dimension split into rows, with sales for two periods and their difference visible together; if a second dimension is needed, it becomes small-multiple panels.
dimension_period_values_and_delta.avoid_when: Avoid when the question is categorical hierarchy, composition, single-period ranking, or continuous trend shape.

ranked_contribution_and_cumulative_share.best_when: The reader needs to identify the few categories that explain most of the metric.
ranked_contribution_and_cumulative_share.avoid_when: Avoid when component composition, period movement, or nested hierarchy is the main message.

summary_exact_values.best_when: The reader needs compact exact AC/PY totals and deltas by comparison row.
summary_exact_values.avoid_when: Avoid when the message depends on the period path or monthly sequencing.

gap_between_two_values.best_when: The reader needs a low-clutter comparison of two values across categories, panels, or periods.
gap_between_two_values.avoid_when: Avoid when the month-by-month path or additive reconciliation matters.

set_overlap.label: Set overlap
set_overlap.description: Show intersections across sets or segments.

many_set_intersections.best_when: The reader needs to compare intersections across more than two sets.
many_set_intersections.avoid_when: Avoid when only two or three simple sets need a familiar Venn view.

intersection_patterns_across_panels.best_when: The reader needs to compare overlap structures across panels or segments.
intersection_patterns_across_panels.avoid_when: Avoid when one aggregate overlap view is enough.

simple_two_or_three_set_overlap.best_when: The reader needs an intuitive overlap picture for two or three sets.
simple_two_or_three_set_overlap.avoid_when: Avoid with more than three sets or many intersections.

time_and_period_movement.label: Time and period movement
time_and_period_movement.description: Understand movement across ordered periods, current-vs-baseline period windows, or period-based summaries.

trend_with_cumulative_or_share_area.best_when: The reader needs a time-series area view for absolute contribution or share over periods.
trend_with_cumulative_or_share_area.avoid_when: Avoid when precise line trajectory or side-by-side period gaps are clearer.

total_metric_by_period_or_scope.best_when: The reader needs a compact total metric column view across selected periods or scopes.
total_metric_by_period_or_scope.avoid_when: Avoid when component composition, relationship, or detailed period path is needed.

dimension_period_values_and_delta.best_when: The reader needs one dimension split into rows, with sales for two periods and their difference visible together; if a second dimension is needed, it becomes small-multiple panels.
dimension_period_values_and_delta.avoid_when: Avoid when the question is categorical hierarchy, composition, single-period ranking, or continuous trend shape.

composition_change_over_periods.best_when: The reader needs to see total and mix composition across periods or comparable scopes.
composition_change_over_periods.avoid_when: Avoid when exact values or simple trend shape matters more than composition.

single_metric_trend_shape.best_when: The reader needs the trend path of the primary mix metric across ordered periods.
single_metric_trend_shape.avoid_when: Avoid when composition, hierarchy, or AC/PY period comparison is the main message.

period_by_period_gap.best_when: The reader needs to compare current and baseline period values across each month or week and see where gaps are largest.
period_by_period_gap.avoid_when: Avoid when the story is only the smooth trajectory shape or a single reconciled total movement.

gap_between_two_values.best_when: The reader needs a low-clutter comparison of two values across categories, panels, or periods.
gap_between_two_values.avoid_when: Avoid when the month-by-month path or additive reconciliation matters.

additive_reconciliation.best_when: The reader needs to see how period variances add from previous total to current total.
additive_reconciliation.avoid_when: Avoid when values are non-additive or the message is trend shape rather than reconciliation.

compact_side_by_side_period_comparison.best_when: The reader needs a compact executive AC/PY period comparison with clear column contrast.
compact_side_by_side_period_comparison.avoid_when: Avoid when line shape, exact values, or total bridge reconciliation is the main message.

endpoint_direction_and_relative_change.best_when: The reader needs direction and magnitude of change between two endpoints, especially across multiple comparable items.
endpoint_direction_and_relative_change.avoid_when: Avoid when intermediate months, exact values, or additive bridge components are essential.

exact_values.best_when: The report needs citeable period values, deltas, and percent deltas rather than a primarily visual reading.
exact_values.avoid_when: Avoid as the only visual when the reader must quickly perceive shape, gap, or reconciliation.

trajectory_shape.best_when: The reader needs to see the ordered period path: acceleration, reversal, narrowing, widening, or seasonality.
trajectory_shape.avoid_when: Avoid when exact values, total reconciliation, or a two-endpoint before/after comparison is the main message.

variance_and_bridge.label: Variance and bridge
variance_and_bridge.description: Reconcile movement through additive drivers, root-cause components, or price-volume-mix decompositions.

additive_reconciliation.best_when: The reader needs to see how period variances add from previous total to current total.
additive_reconciliation.avoid_when: Avoid when values are non-additive or the message is trend shape rather than reconciliation.

parent_bridge_with_child_drilldowns.best_when: Use when the question names one parent grouping dimension and one fixed second decomposition dimension to explain selected parent-row variance moves in the same visual.
parent_bridge_with_child_drilldowns.avoid_when: Avoid when only one dimension is requested, when the second dimension is not meaningful within each parent member, when the task is a variable mixed-dimension root-cause ordering, when PVM mechanics are required, or when exact tabular detail is the deliverable.

pvm_decomposition_comparison.best_when: Use only when the business question explicitly asks how value movement decomposes into price, volume, and mix effects, and the dataset has compatible value, units/volume, and price or derived price semantics.
pvm_decomposition_comparison.avoid_when: Avoid for generic variance explanations, one-dimension splits, root-cause ordering, or any dataset without compatible value, volume, and rate semantics at the same grain.

component_level_root_cause.best_when: Use when the question asks which price, units, and mix component-by-dimension combinations explain movement, rather than which dimensions explain the total variance.
component_level_root_cause.avoid_when: Avoid when units/volume is unavailable, when fewer than two candidate driver dimensions exist, when the report needs the total-variance root-cause sequence, or when a simple PVM ladder is sufficient.

root_cause_path_with_nested_drilldowns.best_when: Use when the question asks for a variable mixed-dimension root-cause variance path and also asks to explain selected root-cause drivers with nested root-cause bridge drilldowns.
root_cause_path_with_nested_drilldowns.avoid_when: Avoid when the question names a fixed parent dimension and fixed child decomposition dimension; use variance.exploded_variance_bridge instead. Avoid when only a one-level root-cause path is needed, or when exact tabular detail is the deliverable.

root_cause_total_movement.best_when: Use when the question asks for the ordered root-cause sequence behind the total movement across available dimensions, and the output should show which driver path explains the overall delta.
root_cause_total_movement.avoid_when: Avoid for a simple total bridge, a one-dimension split, a component-only root-cause question, or PVM decomposition.

scenario_reconciliation.best_when: Use for the plain bridge from one baseline total to one current total when the message is the additive reconciliation itself, not a ranked dimension split, root-cause path, or PVM mechanics.
scenario_reconciliation.avoid_when: Avoid when the question names a dimension to split by, asks for nested drilldowns, asks why via root-cause ordering, asks for price/volume/mix, or when trend shape is the message.

total_delta_split_by_dimension.best_when: Use when the question explicitly asks how the total delta is distributed across members of one named dimension, such as category, retailer, region, brand, or channel.
total_delta_split_by_dimension.avoid_when: Avoid when the reader needs the generic total bridge, a nested parent/child drilldown, an ordered root-cause path across multiple dimensions, or price-volume-mix mechanics.

role_registry.purpose: Canonical mechanical role vocabulary for chart selection. These roles constrain dataset/profile and plugin-parameter matching; they do not decide analysis validity.

period.description: Dataset column that can order or filter observations by time or period.

metric.description: Dataset column or derived metric candidate usable as a numeric measure.

dimension.description: Dataset column usable for grouping, ranking, panels, or composition.

identifier.description: Dataset column that mechanically identifies entities such as products or customers.

direct_dimension.description: Categorical grouping candidate; not a semantic endorsement.

entity_key.description: Entity candidate for cohorts, like-for-like populations, or set membership derivation.

rank_or_lane.description: Rank, lane, band, bucket, or tier candidate.

set_dimension.description: Low-cardinality set/group candidate for overlap analysis.

set_item.description: Item/entity candidate for set membership aggregation.

statement_scenario.description: Scenario column candidate for structured statement values.
